title: Apartments for Rent in Lakewood, OH
metaTitle: Apartments for Rent in Lakewood, Ohio
metaDescription: Looking for apartments for rent in Lakewood, Ohio? See the area's housing stock, transit, typical rent ranges, and how to tell our team what you need.
answerBox: Lakewood, Ohio (ZIP 44107) is one of Cuyahoga County's most densely populated suburbs, with a large stock of early-1900s apartment buildings along the Detroit Avenue and Clifton Boulevard corridors, directly west of Cleveland's Cudell and Edgewater neighborhoods. Greater Cleveland rents typically run $700–$1,800/month. Tell us your budget and we'll point you to comparable options we work with.

body: Lakewood is one of the most densely populated communities in Cuyahoga County, and that density shows up directly in its housing mix: a substantial share of the city's residential buildings are pre-World War II apartment buildings and courtyard-style complexes, concentrated along the Detroit Avenue and Clifton Boulevard corridors, alongside the two-family duplex houses more common on the side streets between them. Most of the city falls within **ZIP code 44107**, directly west of Cleveland's Cudell and Edgewater neighborhoods.

**Rent Finder Cleveland doesn't currently work with apartments in Lakewood.** Our 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods, with a smaller west-side presence. If you're set on an apartment specifically in Lakewood, a Lakewood-based leasing office or listing service will have more direct inventory. If you're open to nearby Cleveland neighborhoods, [tell us your bedroom count and budget](/p/book-showing) and we'll point you to comparable options we do work with.

sections.h2: What do apartments cost across Greater Cleveland?
body: Lakewood-specific apartment pricing varies block by block and building by building, but as a general Greater Cleveland benchmark, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report shows a metro median asking rent of **$1,250/month**, with 1-bedroom units around **$1,195** and 2-bedroom units around **$1,100**. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report, which weights toward larger professionally-managed apartment communities, shows notably higher averages — a pattern that can apply to Lakewood's own larger apartment buildings compared with its smaller courtyard walk-ups.

sections.h2: Getting around Lakewood without a car
body: Lakewood's density means many apartment buildings sit within easy walking distance of the Detroit Avenue retail corridor, and Greater Cleveland RTA bus routes run along Detroit Avenue and Clifton Boulevard into downtown Cleveland — an option apartment renters without a car often rely on more than house renters in lower-density suburbs. I-90 clips the southern edge of the city for anyone driving toward downtown or west toward Rocky River and Westlake, and Lakewood Park runs along the Lake Erie shoreline at the north end of town.

items: **Transit:** RTA bus routes on Detroit Ave and Clifton Blvd; I-90 access
**Location:** Immediately west of Cleveland's Cudell and Edgewater neighborhoods, on Lake Erie
**Housing stock:** Pre-WWII apartment buildings and courtyard complexes, plus two-family duplex houses

sections.h2: Section 8 and vouchers for Lakewood apartments
body: Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept Housing Choice Vouchers, so acceptance for any given Lakewood apartment building comes down to the individual owner rather than a citywide rule. What we can confirm about our own homes: **every property Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts Section 8 vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready**, though our current inventory sits in Cleveland proper, not Lakewood. The [Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) administers the local voucher program countywide, including Lakewood, and accepts preliminary applications year-round. Our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) covers how the process generally works.

sections.h2: Comparing Lakewood apartments with nearby Cleveland neighborhoods
body: If Lakewood is one option among several, our [apartments for rent in Cleveland](/apartments-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) pillar page covers the neighborhoods where we work with inventory directly. Ohio City and Tremont, both a short distance east of Lakewood along the same near-west-side corridor, are worth comparing for similar older-building apartment stock close to transit — see our guides to [Ohio City apartments](/cleveland-rentals/apartments/ohio-city-apartments-for-rent-cleveland/) and [Tremont apartments](/cleveland-rentals/apartments/tremont-apartments-for-rent-cleveland/), or browse the full [suburb-by-suburb guide](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/).

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with apartments in Lakewood, Ohio?
faq.a: Not currently. Our 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods and a smaller west-side presence, not Lakewood. If you're flexible on location, tell us your bedroom count and budget and we'll point you toward comparable options we do work with.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent an apartment in Lakewood, Ohio?
faq.a: Lakewood-specific pricing varies by building, but as a Greater Cleveland benchmark, Zumper's July 2026 report shows a metro median of about $1,195 for 1-bedroom and $1,100 for 2-bedroom units, while RentCafe's July 2026 report shows higher averages for larger professionally-managed buildings.

faq.q: What ZIP code is Lakewood, Ohio in?
faq.a: Lakewood is primarily ZIP code 44107, sitting on Lake Erie directly west of Cleveland's Cudell and Edgewater neighborhoods.

faq.q: Do Lakewood apartment buildings accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: It depends on the individual building owner — Ohio has no statewide law requiring voucher acceptance. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, though our current homes are concentrated in Cleveland rather than Lakewood. CMHA administers the local voucher program countywide.

faq.q: What's the apartment stock like in Lakewood?
faq.a: Lakewood has a large share of pre-World War II apartment buildings and courtyard-style complexes concentrated along the Detroit Avenue and Clifton Boulevard corridors, reflecting its status as one of the most densely populated suburbs in Cuyahoga County, alongside its well-known two-family duplex houses.
